questions.question: What does a two-way table help to analyze?
In a two-way table, what is $\frac{20}{100}$ called?
A hospital recorded $40$ patients with disease A and $60$ patients with disease B. What is the relative frequency of patients with disease A?
What can be determined using a two-way table?

options.A: Only the relationship between two variables
options.B: Only the median of one variable
options.D: Only the mode of one variable
options.E: Only the range of one variable

questions.question: A pharmaceutical company conducted an experiment with two types of medicine, X and Y. The results are shown in the table below. What is the relative frequency of patients who took medicine X and showed improvement?
Two-way tables can be used to analyze the relationship between which of the following?
A study on the effect of vitamin D on bone health collected data on the number of people with and without vitamin D deficiency. What can be calculated using this data?

options.A: Only the mean of one variable
options.B: Only the median of one variable
options.C: The relative risk of vitamin D deficiency
options.D: Only the mode of one variable
options.E: Only the range of one variable

questions.question: What is an example of a two-way table in healthcare?
Use the two-way table below to calculate the relative frequency of patients who took medicine A and showed improvement. Show your work and explain your answer.

A researcher collected data on the relationship between exercise and heart disease. The results are shown in the table below. Analyze the association between exercise and heart disease. Use $\frac{a}{b}$ format to show relative frequencies and explain your conclusions.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Use real-world examples to illustrate the concepts
Tip 2: Encourage students to show their work and explain their answers
Tip 3: Emphasize the importance of understanding the context of the problem
